summary: Completed backend-only regression verification for the Android control-path compile fix. Confirmed android bridge no longer uses unavailable JUCE convertTo0to1 API, sendParameter uses manual endpoint normalization, normalization table covers all Main.cmajor value endpoints, and template script/native path checks remain green.
action_items: Run the GitHub Android build once to validate compile success on runner after template generation.
Optionally add a device/emulator smoke run to validate runtime parameter behavior beyond static/template regression checks.
critical_code_review_comments: Previous regression test expected removed API convertTo0to1 and was stale; updated assertions now match current manual normalization implementation.
No remaining critical defects found in requested files for this compile-fix scope.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Added three targeted tests: (1) assert no convertTo0to1 usage in android_bridge.cpp, (2) assert sendParameter uses normaliseParameterValue before setValueNotifyingHost, (3) assert normalization table covers all Main.cmajor input value endpoints. Existing template/script path and JS syntax regressions remain passing.
rca of the issue: Root cause was reliance on AudioProcessorParameter::convertTo0to1 in android_bridge.cpp, which is unavailable for this JUCE API surface in CI build context. Compile-safe mitigation is manual per-endpoint normalization using Main.cmajor ranges, then passing normalized values to setValueNotifyingHost. No MOCKED APIs used.
